title: USD Relocates Namespace Source Contract

included: wrapping a base NamespaceSource with direct layerRelocates application
reading source-visible layerRelocates from the base source root/layer fields
empty relocate target removing the source subtree from the composed scene graph
validation for root and pseudo-root relocates, non-prim paths, duplicate sources or targets, identity mappings, target-below-source mappings, and target-at-source-ancestor mappings
target local opinions stronger than relocated source opinions
recoverable diagnostics for local-layer-stack opinions authored at relocate source paths while retaining otherwise-valid relocate entries
effective population specifier selection across target-local and relocated source specs
ordered child prim name composition with relocated source children projected to target names
ordered property name composition across target-local and relocated source specs
recoverable diagnostics for nested relocate sources authored beneath an already-relocated ancestral source path
relocate namespace mappings authored in referenced or payloaded layer stacks as inputs to the arc providers that introduced those layer stacks
deferred: automatic source-path normalization/rewrite suggestions beyond diagnosing ancestral relocated path authoring errors
composing relocates into inherit and specialize namespace mappings during recursive arc evaluation
inherits, specializes, recursive variants, and value clips
attribute value resolution and time sample interpolation

model.RelocatesNamespaceSource: A namespace-source provider backed by a composed non-variant base NamespaceSource that applies direct layerRelocates path mappings.
model.BaseNamespaceSource: The composed namespace input to be relocated. In the current bounded graph this is produced by usd-reference-namespace-source; usd-composition-arbitrator is the terminal provider that can merge relocated opinions with loaded payload opinions.
model.RelocateEntry: A source prim path mapped to an optional target prim path. An empty target removes the source subtree from the composed scene graph.
model.RelocateMapping: The source subtree at and under the relocate source path projected onto the target path by replacing the source path prefix with the target path.
model.ContributingSources: Adapter rendering of strong-to-weak ContributingOpinion records. Domain records store source identity separately from semantic spec paths.

input.baseSource: target-native NamespaceSource exposing layerRelocates and composed prim/property opinions

output.relocates: direct relocate entries with sourcePath, targetPath, and deleted

output.exists: whether target-local or relocated source specs contribute a prim-like input at the path
output.childNames: composed child prim names before stage mask or active filtering
output.propertyNames: composed property names before schema fallback injection
output.contributingSources: strong-to-weak target-local or relocated source contributors

output.exists: whether target-local or relocated source specs contribute an attribute or relationship at the path
output.fields: strongest authored field per field key
output.contributingSources: strong-to-weak target-local or relocated source contributors

input.path: absolute composed prim path, variant-selection prim path, or /

output.opinions: strong-to-weak contributing opinions after direct relocates have been applied

laws: the relocates source consumes a base NamespaceSource and must not require stage population to know whether opinions came from base layers, references, payloads, or relocates
the relocates source must not open files, resolve assets, or inspect parser-local USDA syntax
relocates are read from the source-visible layerRelocates field on the composed base root/layer input
relocate source records must retain source paths, target paths, and mapped contributor paths as semantic path handles/references rather than raw strings
relocates contributingSources domain data must be typed ContributingOpinion records, not composite source:path strings
relocate source-path opinion checks must compare typed source identifiers and semantic source paths, not parse contributor display strings
a relocate source path and authored target path must be prim paths and must not be the pseudo-root or root prim path
an empty relocate target removes the source subtree from the composed namespace instead of mapping it to another path
each non-empty source path must have only one target, and each non-empty target path must have only one source
a relocate target must not be the same as its source
a relocate target must not be below its source path
a relocate target must not place a prim at the path of an existing ancestor of the source path

source-local authored opinions at the relocate source path are a recoverable composition error: the source must diagnose them, ignore those source-local opinions, and retain/apply the otherwise-valid relocate entry

a relocate source path authored below an already-relocated source must be diagnosed as a recoverable composition error because the authored source path is not the ancestral relocated path; the invalid nested relocate entry must be ignored while other valid relocate entries continue composing

references and payloads authored inside a layer stack with layerRelocates must receive an additional arc-local namespace mapping from that layer stack; generated implementations must apply that mapping in the reference or payload provider rather than expecting stage population to relocate already-populated prims

arc-local relocate mappings must hide relocated source paths, delete source subtrees for empty targets, preserve target-local opinions as stronger than relocated source opinions, and redirect remote source reads to the relocated source specs for nested reference or payload layer stacks

path-valued fields emitted through an arc-local relocate mapping, including targetPaths and connectionPaths, must be remapped to the exposed relocated target path or pruned when the target falls inside a deleted relocate source subtree

when the consumed base source has already combined local and reference-family opinions, the relocates source must consume separable reference-family-only source opinions or equivalent per-family provenance for the relocate source path instead of relocating a flattened local+remote field map
for this bounded direct provider, target-local opinions are stronger than relocated source opinions
the source subtree maps to the target subtree, and descendants are mapped by replacing the source prefix with the target prefix
properties under a relocated source prim map to the corresponding relocated target prim
the source path and its descendants are hidden from composed scene traversal after a non-empty relocate
relocated source opinions are applied at relocates LIVERPS strength: weaker than local, inherits, and variants, and stronger than references, payloads, and specializes in the full composition model

the concrete graph currently evaluates relocates after the reference provider so the provider can see referenced source opinions that need to be moved; usd-composition-arbitrator may merge payload opinions with relocated opinions later, and evaluation order must not be read as field-strength order
for non-special field keys in this first contract, field maps expose the strongest authored field value and do not combine dictionaries, listOps, time samples, or values
childNames returned by this source are already composition-ordered; stage population must preserve this order for sources declaring child_ordering=composed
propertyNames returned by this source are already composition-ordered; stage population must preserve this order for sources declaring property_ordering=composed
adapter JSON may seed base, sublayer, and reference layers for conformance tests, but domain APIs exchange NamespaceSource, path, token, and field value objects

adapter_protocol.input: A JSON file containing one case with rootLayer, optional sublayers map/list, optional referenceLayers map, and an ops array. The adapter constructs the configured base namespace-source chain before constructing the relocates namespace source.
adapter_protocol.output: A JSON object with ok, diagnostics, and results.
